description: Falsifiable thesis claims under practitioner test. Each claim has a flag-based qualitative accept/reject criterion (pre-registered process, not a fragile percentage gate). Evaluated against survey + interview evidence at small N (counts + quotes, not population means).
conflict_of_interest: The authors of this instrument built the system under evaluation. This is disclosed to every respondent and in the analysis write-up.

claims.statement: Real production cascades exist where the loudest alert fires on a downstream victim, not the root cause.
claims.accept_if: At least 3 independent on-call SREs describe a real incident matching this pattern in free text.
claims.reject_if: Fewer than 2 SREs recognize the pattern, or majority say it is contrived/rare.

claims.statement: There exist incidents where the obvious/naive remediation makes the outage worse (a trap).
claims.accept_if: At least 3 independent SREs give a concrete example of a naive fix that worsened an incident.
claims.reject_if: Majority say naive fixes essentially never backfire in their experience.

claims.statement: Practitioners independently rank resolution quality as: clean root-cause fix > symptom-suppressed-but-cause-live > trap (made-worse), matching our reward tiers (1.0 > 0.45 > -0.60).
claims.accept_if: Open-elicited ordering from at least 3 SREs is consistent with our tier ordering BEFORE our tiers are revealed.
claims.reject_if: Multiple SREs order outcomes differently (e.g. value 'back up fast' over root-cause) in a way that contradicts the reward sign structure.
claims.review_if: Mixed orderings; document the disagreement and which scenarios it applies to.

claims.statement: Our concrete CIDG scenarios (e.g. redis-cache-flush, auth-cert-expiry, billing-disk-fill) read as plausible real incidents, not toy puzzles.
claims.accept_if: For a given scenario, 0 or 1 SRE flags it unrealistic.
claims.reject_if: A scenario is flagged unrealistic by >=2 independent SREs -> mandatory documented review of that scenario.
claims.review_if: Any scenario flagged by exactly 2; review and document outcome.

claims.statement: An autonomous agent that correctly identifies root cause + safe fix + avoids the trap would be trusted/useful to a real on-call SRE.
claims.accept_if: Majority express conditional trust (would use as a triage co-pilot) AND name the conditions.
claims.reject_if: Majority say they would not trust/act on such an agent under any near-term condition.
claims.review_if: Split; document the stated trust conditions for the backlog.
